task_objective.description: Design a simulator to generate hospital state trajectories for a Three-disease Hospital Environment. A trajectory is an ordered sequence of state dictionaries over time: {state[0], state[1], ..., state[T]} where each state contains hospital bed occupancy and the current in-hospital patient list.
task_objective.simulation_focus:
Goal: generate state transition sequences from initial conditions using a parameterized SimulatorStep class.
Key components:
(1) Define hospital flow parameters (arrivals, triage/bed assignment, LOS distributions, discharge dynamics) as model parameters.
(2) Implement step(state[t], rng) that computes state[t+1] from state[t] using stochastic hospital dynamics. There is NO direct external action; each day proceeds as:
    1) New patients arrive
    2) The environment attempts to allocate each new arrival to an ICU or Standard bed under capacity/rules
    3) If no suitable bed is available, the patient is not admitted
(3) Parameters are optimized via Simulation-Based Inference (SBI) using NPE to fit observed training trajectories.
(4) The simulator must enforce bed capacity constraints and keep occupancy consistent with the patient list.
(5) The observed training dataset has very few samples; the model must generalize to unseen trajectories (validation/test).

Implementation note (matches the provided HOSP SBI pipeline): the SBI observation and simulator outputs are NOT the raw patient list; instead they use a fixed 7-D per-time-step summary produced by trajectories_to_numpy().
task_objective.Data Calibration Procedure:
Calibration loop (parameter optimization):
(1) Load train/val/test trajectories from CSV files (format: trajectory_id, time_step, day, icu_occupancy, standard_occupancy, patients).
(2) SBI training uses ONLY train_states[0] (the first trajectory, trajectory_id=0) as the single observation.
    Observation vector construction (exactly matches HOSP trajectories_to_numpy):
    - Extract train_states[0] as a list of state dictionaries for t=0..T.
    - Convert this single trajectory to a numpy array via trajectories_to_numpy(train_states[0]).
      This produces an array of shape (T+1, 7) where the 7 features at each time step are:
        [day,
         icu_occupancy,
         standard_occupancy,
         disease0_alive_count,
         disease1_alive_count,
         disease2_alive_count,
         total_alive_count]
      where disease*_alive_count and total_alive_count are computed by iterating st["patients"] and counting only patients with is_alive==True.
    - Flatten across time to build the SBI observation:
      x_o = flatten(observed_trajectory_np) and reshape to (1, (T+1)*7).
(3) Simulated observation generation for NPE training (exactly matches HOSP _simulation_wrapper behavior):
    - Sample parameters θ_i from a BoxUniform prior constructed from Model().get_parameters_uniform_prior_min_max().
    - For each θ_i, set Model(θ_i) and simulate a predicted trajectory starting from the initial state of train_states[0] (i.e., the time_step=0 state of the observation trajectory) for horizon T.
    - Convert the predicted trajectory to the same (T+1, 7) representation using trajectories_to_numpy(predicted_trajectory) and flatten to x_i with shape (1, (T+1)*7).
    - Collect pairs (θ_i, x_i) for i=1..N for training NPE.
(4) Train Neural Posterior Estimation (NPE):
    - Train a conditional density estimator p(θ|x) using the simulated pairs (θ_i, x_i).
(5) Posterior inference:
    - Condition on the real observation x_o (from train_states[0]) and sample θ* ~ p(θ|x_o).
    - Use the posterior mean as optimized parameters: θ_optimized = mean(θ* samples).
(6) Evaluation (full datasets, matches HOSP evaluate_simulator_code_using_sbi):
    - Using θ_optimized, evaluate on ALL trajectories in train/val/test:
      * compute overall MSE on train, val, and test via _compute_all_losses_with_states (which internally creates predicted trajectories and compares to true trajectories in the same (T+1,7) representation)
      * compute validation per-dimension MSE over the 7 dimensions via _compute_mse_per_dimension_with_states
      * if enabled by config/full flag, also compute distributional metrics such as MMD and Wasserstein (do not change their computation).
NOTE: SBI training uses only train_states[0] as observation and as the simulation starting point inside the SBI simulator wrapper, but final evaluation uses all trajectories in train/val/test for robust assessment.
task_objective.Simulator Design Requirements:
Build a SimulatorStep class that implements daily (discrete-time) stochastic hospital flow dynamics.

CRITICAL SCOPE:
- State format (must match data semantics):
  {
    "day": int,
    "icu_occupancy": int,
    "standard_occupancy": int,
    "patients": list[{
      "disease_id": int in {0,1,2},
      "bed_type": "ICU"|"Standard",
      "los_remaining": int,
      "is_alive": bool,
      "day_in_hospital": int
    }]
  }
- Trajectory length: T derived from max time_step in each trajectory (typically 60, i.e., 61 rows from 0..60).
- There is NO external action. The transition is driven by: daily arrivals, bed assignment, LOS decrement, discharge.

Must implement:
1) __init__ (define parameters and any fixed capacities).
2) step(...) (state transition; ignore action if present in skeleton).
3) get_parameters(), set_parameters(), get_parameters_uniform_prior_min_max() (for SBI).
4) Internal consistency constraints:
   - Occupancies should be consistent with the patient list bed_type counts (ICU vs Standard) for admitted/alive patients.
   - Bed capacities must be enforced (ICU and Standard).
   - los_remaining decreases daily for admitted patients; discharge when los_remaining <= 0.
   - day increments by 1 each step (and should align with time_step semantics in the data).
5) Stochasticity: allowed/encouraged via numpy RNG; ensure categorical probabilities are normalized inside step().
6) Code must be interpretable and fit the dataset as accurately as possible.

Observation/metric interface requirement (important for SBI + evaluation):
- The SBI observation x and the evaluation losses are computed over the 7-D summary per time step produced by trajectories_to_numpy():
  [day, icu_occupancy, standard_occupancy, d0_alive, d1_alive, d2_alive, total_alive].
- Your simulator must produce states whose patients/occupancy fields lead to realistic values for these 7 dimensions when passed through trajectories_to_numpy().
task_objective.Experimental settings:
ONLY ONE setting is allowed:
- Synthetic hospital trajectories generated from a ground-truth hospital simulator with fixed but unknown parameters.

Data splits:
- Training set: 100 trajectories (train_seed_*_n_100.csv).
- Validation set: 100 trajectories (val_seed_*_n_100.csv).
- Test set: 100 trajectories (test_seed_*_n_100.csv).

Optimization method:
- SBI: Neural Posterior Estimation (NPE) using train_states[0] (trajectory_id=0) as the single observation x_o; then evaluate θ_optimized on all trajectories.

No temporal holdout split is required beyond the provided train/val/test files. Do not introduce additional within-trajectory splits.
task_objective.Input-Output Mapping of the Simulated Ag:
Input: current state dictionary state[t] with fields (day, icu_occupancy, standard_occupancy, patients).
Output: next state dictionary state[t+1] computed via the hospital flow dynamics.
Pipeline: For each trajectory, start from state[time_step=0] as initial condition, then sequentially apply step(state[t], rng) to predict state[t+1] for t=0,...,T-1.
Evaluation: The system converts true and predicted trajectories into numpy arrays using trajectories_to_numpy(), yielding shape (num_trajectories, T+1, 7), then computes losses:
- overall MSE on train/val/test
- validation per-dimension MSE for the 7 dimensions (day, ICU occ, Standard occ, d0_alive, d1_alive, d2_alive, total_alive)
- optionally MMD and Wasserstein if enabled by configuration.

data_files.train_seed_10_n_100.csv: Hospital environment training trajectories (100 independent trajectories). CSV columns: trajectory_id (0-99), time_step (0-60), day (0-60; duplicates time_step), icu_occupancy (int), standard_occupancy (int), patients (JSON string of a list). Each patient dict has keys: disease_id in {0,1,2}, bed_type in {"ICU","Standard"}, los_remaining (int), is_alive (bool), day_in_hospital (int). IMPORTANT: For SBI training, ONLY use the first trajectory (train_states[0], trajectory_id=0) as observation x_o and as the rollout starting point inside the SBI simulator wrapper. Final evaluation uses ALL trajectories from train/val/test.
data_files.val_seed_10_n_100.csv: Hospital environment validation trajectories. Same schema/columns as training. Used for validation loss reporting and per-dimension MSE (7 dimensions).
data_files.test_seed_10_n_100.csv: Hospital environment test trajectories. Same schema/columns as training. Used ONLY for final evaluation after all training/selection.

mse.description:
Mean Squared Error between generated and ground-truth trajectories computed over the 7-D per-time-step summary produced by trajectories_to_numpy(). The 7 dimensions are:
[day, icu_occupancy, standard_occupancy, disease0_alive, disease1_alive, disease2_alive, total_alive].
MSE is averaged over all time steps and trajectories in a dataset split. Lower is better.

mse_per_dimension.description:
Per-dimension MSE computed separately for each of the 7 dimensions from trajectories_to_numpy(). Returns a dictionary keyed by these dimensions (or an ordered list mapped to them):
{"day": mse_day, "icu_occupancy": mse_icu, "standard_occupancy": mse_std, "d0_alive": mse_d0, "d1_alive": mse_d1, "d2_alive": mse_d2, "total_alive": mse_total}.
Lower is better for each dimension.

train_loss.description: Loss metrics (overall MSE, and optional distributional metrics if enabled) computed on training trajectories (ALL 100 trajectories), using θ_optimized inferred from SBI on train_states[0].

val_loss.description: Loss metrics (overall MSE, per-dimension MSE, and optional MMD/Wasserstein if enabled) computed on validation trajectories (ALL 100 trajectories), using θ_optimized inferred from SBI on train_states[0].

test_loss.description: Loss metrics (overall MSE, and optional distributional metrics if enabled) computed on test trajectories (ALL 100 trajectories), using θ_optimized inferred from SBI on train_states[0]. Used ONLY for final performance assessment.
